assumption_text: The full-estimate episode uses the national territory of Cambodia under Democratic Kampuchea rather than a subnational theater.
rationale: The strongest direct-death envelope, mass-grave inventory, and population reconstructions are regime-wide, so a national denominator is more defensible than a false subnational precision.

assumption_text: Most civilians physically present in Cambodia remained exposed inside the country during the regime; PPR-era outflow is not modeled as a large monthly drain.
rationale: Heuveline 2015 states that leaving Cambodia during the Pol Pot regime was extremely difficult and that the bulk of refugee flight occurred before or especially after the regime.

assumption_text: The early 1975 expulsion of roughly 150,000 ethnic Vietnamese is treated as an immediate denominator correction rather than ignored.
rationale: Kiernan and Do both indicate a large early removal of ethnic Vietnamese residents from Cambodia after the Khmer Rouge takeover.

assumption_text: The January 7, 1979 civilian population is back-cast from the January 1, 1980 population of 6.4 million using a 0 to 0.5 million 1979 famine offset; the best scenario uses +0.3 million.
rationale: The episode ends one year before the demographic endpoint in Heuveline 2001, so a back-cast is unavoidable.

assumption_text: Military person-months are interpolated from 68k (April 1975), 72,248 (January 1976), and >100k (October 1978), producing scenario-average force levels of about 72k / 84k / 96k.
rationale: No continuous roster exists in the consulted sources, but these anchors bracket plausible regular-force exposure.

assumption_text: Remnant FANK exposure and foreign-force exposure are not fully reconstructed in the military denominator.
rationale: Available force anchors are much better for the Khmer Rouge than for surviving Khmer Republic forces or all Vietnamese military exposure inside Cambodia.

assumption_text: A large unknown-status bucket is retained and not silently allocated to civilians or military/combatants.
rationale: S-21 and many execution-site sources explicitly mix party members, former officials, soldiers, civilians, and children.

assumption_text: The total direct-violent-death envelope is set at 500k / 850k / 1.1m rather than using the 2.2m excess-mortality figure.
rationale: Core DR is defined on direct violent deaths only; excess mortality mixes famine, disease, and other indirect deaths.

assumption_text: DR low/best/high use coherent scenario bundles rather than all-pairs extreme combinations.
rationale: All-pairs extremes would generate internally implausible ratios from incompatible numerator and denominator assumptions.
